1	package fizeau
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"

13		codexharness "github.com/easel/fizeau/internal/harnesses/codex"
14		geminiharness "github.com/easel/fizeau/internal/harnesses/gemini"
15		"github.com/easel/fizeau/internal/routehealth"
16		"github.com/easel/fizeau/internal/serviceimpl"
17		sessionusage "github.com/easel/fizeau/internal/session"
18	)
19	
20	// FizeauService is the entire public Go surface of the fizeau module.
21	// See CONTRACT-003 for the full specification.
22	type FizeauService interface {
23		Execute(ctx context.Context, req ServiceExecuteRequest) (<-chan ServiceEvent, error)
24		TailSessionLog(ctx context.Context, sessionID string) (<-chan ServiceEvent, error)
25		ListHarnesses(ctx context.Context) ([]HarnessInfo, error)
26		ListProviders(ctx context.Context) ([]ProviderInfo, error)
27		ListModels(ctx context.Context, filter ModelFilter) ([]ModelInfo, error)
28		ListPolicies(ctx context.Context) ([]PolicyInfo, error)
29		HealthCheck(ctx context.Context, health HealthTarget) error
30		ResolveRoute(ctx context.Context, req RouteRequest) (*RouteDecision, error)
31		RecordRouteAttempt(ctx context.Context, attempt RouteAttempt) error
32		RouteStatus(ctx context.Context) (*RouteStatusReport, error)
33	
34		// Historical session-log projections. CONTRACT-003 owns these so CLI
35		// subcommands such as log/replay/usage do not need to read the
36		// internal session-log JSONL schema.
37		UsageReport(ctx context.Context, opts UsageReportOptions) (*UsageReport, error)
38		ListSessionLogs(ctx context.Context) ([]SessionLogEntry, error)
39		WriteSessionLog(ctx context.Context, sessionID string, w io.Writer) error
40		ReplaySession(ctx context.Context, sessionID string, w io.Writer) error
41	}
42	
43	// ServiceConfig provides provider and routing data to the service without
44	// creating an import cycle from the root package into agent/config.
45	// Callers wrap their loaded *config.Config in a type that satisfies this interface.
46	type ServiceConfig interface {
47		// ProviderNames returns provider names in stable order (default first).
48		ProviderNames() []string
49		// DefaultProviderName returns the name of the configured default provider.
50		DefaultProviderName() string
51		// Provider returns the raw config values for a named provider.
52		Provider(name string) (ServiceProviderEntry, bool)
53		// HealthCooldown returns the configured cooldown duration (0 = use default 30s).
54		HealthCooldown() time.Duration
55		// WorkDir is the base directory for file-backed health state.
56		WorkDir() string
57		// SessionLogDir returns the configured sessions directory.
58		SessionLogDir() string
59	}
60	
61	// ServiceProviderEntry carries the minimal provider data the service needs.
62	type ServiceProviderEntry struct {
63		Type           string // "openai" | "openrouter" | "lmstudio" | "llama-server" | "ds4" | "omlx" | "lucebox" | "vllm" | "rapid-mlx" | "ollama" | "anthropic"
64		BaseURL        string
65		ServerInstance string
66		Endpoints      []ServiceProviderEndpoint
67		APIKey         string
68		Headers        map[string]string
69		Model          string // configured default model (may be empty)
70		Billing        BillingModel
71		// IncludeByDefault reports whether this provider participates in automatic
72		// routing when the caller does not pin a provider.
73		IncludeByDefault    bool
74		IncludeByDefaultSet bool
75		// ContextWindow is the configured provider-side context override.
76		ContextWindow int
77		// ConfigError marks this provider entry invalid while allowing the rest of
78		// the service config to load. Invalid providers are reported in status
79		// surfaces and excluded from routing.
80		ConfigError string
81		// DailyTokenBudget is the operator-configured per-UTC-day token budget
82		// (request + response) for this provider. Zero means no local burn-rate
83		// prediction; the provider's own quota signal still applies.
84		DailyTokenBudget int
85	}
86	
87	// ServiceProviderEndpoint is one configured provider serving endpoint.
88	type ServiceProviderEndpoint struct {
89		Name           string
90		BaseURL        string
91		ServerInstance string
92	}
93	
94	// ServiceOptions configures a FizeauService instance.
95	//
96	// seamOptions is embedded so production builds (no testseam tag) get an
97	// empty struct — making it a compile-time error to set seam fields without
98	// the build tag. Test builds inherit the four CONTRACT-003 seams
99	// (FakeProvider, PromptAssertionHook, CompactionAssertionHook,
100	// ToolWiringHook) automatically.
101	type ServiceOptions struct {
102		seamOptions
103	
104		ConfigPath string    // optional override; default $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/fizeau/config.yaml
105		Logger     io.Writer // optional; agent writes structured session logs internally regardless
106	
107		// ServiceConfig, when non-nil, supplies provider and routing data for
108		// ListProviders and HealthCheck. Pass a value wrapping the loaded config.
109		// When nil, those methods return an error.
110		ServiceConfig ServiceConfig
111	
112		// QuotaRefreshDebounce is the minimum interval between live quota probes for
113		// a primary subscription harness. Zero uses the service default.
114		QuotaRefreshDebounce time.Duration
115		// QuotaRefreshStartupWait bounds startup waiting when the durable quota
116		// cache is missing, stale, or incomplete. Zero uses the service default.
117		QuotaRefreshStartupWait time.Duration
118		// QuotaRefreshInterval enables periodic refresh for long-running server
119		// processes. Zero disables the timer; cache refresh still happens on startup
120		// and service activity.
121		QuotaRefreshInterval time.Duration
122		// QuotaRefreshContext optionally cancels the periodic server refresh worker.
123		// When nil, the worker uses context.Background().

398	// ValidateCachePolicy returns nil when v is one of the accepted CachePolicy
399	// values ("", "default", "off") and a typed error otherwise. The empty
400	// string is treated as "default".
401	func ValidateCachePolicy(v string) error {
402		switch v {
403		case "", CachePolicyDefault, CachePolicyOff:
404			return nil
405		default:
406			return fmt.Errorf("invalid CachePolicy %q: want \"\", %q, or %q", v, CachePolicyDefault, CachePolicyOff)
407		}
408	}

410	// ValidatePowerBounds returns nil when the optional numeric routing power
411	// bounds are unset or coherent. Zero means "unset"; positive values are on
412	// the catalog's 1-10 power scale.
413	func ValidatePowerBounds(minPower, maxPower int) error {
414		if minPower < 0 {
415			return fmt.Errorf("invalid MinPower %d: must be >= 0", minPower)
416		}
417		if maxPower < 0 {
418			return fmt.Errorf("invalid MaxPower %d: must be >= 0", maxPower)
419		}
420		if minPower > 0 && maxPower > 0 && maxPower < minPower {
421			return fmt.Errorf("invalid power bounds: MaxPower %d must be >= MinPower %d", maxPower, minPower)
422		}
423		return nil
424	}

936	// loadServiceConfig, when non-nil, is called by New to load a ServiceConfig
937	// from a directory path when opts.ServiceConfig is nil. It is registered by
938	// the config package via init() to break the import cycle (config imports root).
939	var loadServiceConfig func(dir string) (ServiceConfig, error)
940	
941	// RegisterConfigLoader is called by the config package's init() to install the
942	// config-loading function. Do not call this from application code.
943	func RegisterConfigLoader(fn func(dir string) (ServiceConfig, error)) {
944		loadServiceConfig = fn
945	}

947	// New constructs a FizeauService. When opts.ServiceConfig is nil, New attempts to
948	// load configuration automatically:
949	//  1. If opts.ConfigPath is non-empty, load from filepath.Dir(opts.ConfigPath).
950	//  2. Otherwise, load from the default global config location.
951	//
952	// Automatic loading requires the config package to be imported somewhere in the
953	// binary (it registers the loader via init). If the config package is not
954	// imported and ServiceConfig is nil, the service starts without provider config
955	// (ListProviders/HealthCheck will return errors until config is injected).
956	func New(opts ServiceOptions) (FizeauService, error) {
957		if opts.ServiceConfig == nil && loadServiceConfig != nil && shouldAutoLoadServiceConfig(opts) {
958			dir := ""
959			if opts.ConfigPath != "" {
960				dir = filepath.Dir(opts.ConfigPath)
961			}
962			sc, err := loadServiceConfig(dir)
963			if err != nil {
964				return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent.New: load config: %w", err)
965			}
966			opts.ServiceConfig = sc
967		}
968		svc := &service{
969			opts:             opts,
970			registry:         harnesses.NewRegistry(),
971			hub:              newSessionHub(),
972			runtime:          serviceimpl.NewRuntime(serviceimpl.RuntimeDeps{}),
973			catalog:          newCatalogCache(catalogCacheOptions{AsyncRefreshTimeout: opts.catalogRefreshTimeout()}),
974			routeHealth:      routehealth.NewStore(),
975			routeLeases:      routehealth.NewLeaseStore(),
976			routeUtilization: routehealth.NewUtilizationStore(),
977			routingQuality:   newRoutingQualityStore(),
978			providerQuota:    NewProviderQuotaStateStore(),
979			providerBurnRate: NewProviderBurnRateTracker(),
980		}
981		// Hydrate per-provider daily_token_budget from ServiceConfig so the
982		// burn-rate tracker can predict exhaustion before the upstream quota
983		// signal arrives.
984		if opts.ServiceConfig != nil {
985			for _, name := range opts.ServiceConfig.ProviderNames() {
986				entry, ok := opts.ServiceConfig.Provider(name)
987				if !ok {
988					continue
989				}
990				if entry.DailyTokenBudget > 0 {
991					svc.providerBurnRate.SetBudget(name, entry.DailyTokenBudget)
992				}
993			}
994		}
995		svc.reapStaleHarnessSessions()
996		svc.ensurePrimaryQuotaRefresh(context.Background(), quotaRefreshStartup)
997		svc.startPrimaryQuotaRefreshWorker()
998		svc.startQuotaRecoveryProbeLoop()
999		return svc, nil
1000	}

1030	// claudeQuotaState reads the durable Claude quota cache and converts it to QuotaState.
1031	func claudeQuotaState() *QuotaState {
1032		snap, ok := claudeharness.ReadClaudeQuota()
1033		if !ok || snap == nil {
1034			source, err := claudeharness.ClaudeQuotaCachePath()
1035			if err != nil {
1036				source = "claude quota cache"
1037			}
1038			return unavailableQuotaState(source, "claude quota cache unavailable")
1039		}
1040		now := time.Now()
1041		decision := claudeharness.DecideClaudeQuotaRouting(snap, now, 0)
1042		qs := &QuotaState{
1043			CapturedAt: snap.CapturedAt,
1044			Fresh:      decision.Fresh,
1045			Source:     snap.Source,
1046		}
1047		if len(snap.Windows) > 0 {
1048			qs.Windows = append(qs.Windows, snap.Windows...)
1049		} else if snap.FiveHourLimit > 0 {
1050			var used float64
1051			if snap.FiveHourLimit > 0 {
1052				used = float64(snap.FiveHourLimit-snap.FiveHourRemaining) / float64(snap.FiveHourLimit) * 100
1053			}
1054			qs.Windows = append(qs.Windows, harnesses.QuotaWindow{
1055				Name:          "5h",
1056				WindowMinutes: 300,
1057				UsedPercent:   used,
1058				State:         harnesses.QuotaStateFromUsedPercent(int(used)),
1059			})
1060		}
1061		if len(snap.Windows) == 0 && snap.WeeklyLimit > 0 {
1062			var used float64
1063			if snap.WeeklyLimit > 0 {
1064				used = float64(snap.WeeklyLimit-snap.WeeklyRemaining) / float64(snap.WeeklyLimit) * 100
1065			}
1066			qs.Windows = append(qs.Windows, harnesses.QuotaWindow{
1067				Name:          "7d",
1068				WindowMinutes: 10080,
1069				UsedPercent:   used,
1070				State:         harnesses.QuotaStateFromUsedPercent(int(used)),
1071			})
1072		}
1073		qs.Status = quotaStatus(qs.Fresh, qs.Windows)
1074		return qs
1075	}

1181	func (s *service) harnessUsageWindow(provider, since string) *UsageWindow {
1182		logDir := s.serviceSessionLogDir()
1183		if logDir == "" {
1184			return nil
1185		}
1186		now := s.now()
1187		report, err := sessionusage.AggregateUsage(logDir, sessionusage.UsageOptions{Since: since, Now: now})
1188		if err != nil || report == nil {
1189			return nil
1190		}
1191		var total sessionusage.UsageRow
1192		for _, row := range report.Rows {
1193			if row.Provider != provider {
1194				continue
1195			}
1196			total.Sessions += row.Sessions
1197			total.SuccessSessions += row.SuccessSessions
1198			total.FailedSessions += row.FailedSessions
1199			total.InputTokens += row.InputTokens
1200			total.OutputTokens += row.OutputTokens
1201			total.TotalTokens += row.TotalTokens
1202			total.CacheReadTokens += row.CacheReadTokens
1203			total.CacheWriteTokens += row.CacheWriteTokens
1204			total.UnknownCostSessions += row.UnknownCostSessions
1205			if row.KnownCostUSD == nil || total.UnknownCostSessions > 0 {
1206				total.KnownCostUSD = nil
1207			} else {
1208				if total.KnownCostUSD == nil {
1209					total.KnownCostUSD = new(float64)
1210				}
1211				*total.KnownCostUSD += *row.KnownCostUSD
1212			}
1213		}
1214		if total.Sessions == 0 {
1215			return nil
1216		}
1217		window := UsageWindow{
1218			Name:                since,
1219			Source:              logDir,
1220			CapturedAt:          now,
1221			Fresh:               true,
1222			InputTokens:         total.InputTokens,
1223			OutputTokens:        total.OutputTokens,
1224			TotalTokens:         total.TotalTokens,
1225			CacheReadTokens:     total.CacheReadTokens,
1226			CacheWriteTokens:    total.CacheWriteTokens,
1227			KnownCostUSD:        total.KnownCostUSD,
1228			UnknownCostSessions: total.UnknownCostSessions,
1229		}
1230		if total.KnownCostUSD != nil {
1231			window.CostUSD = *total.KnownCostUSD
1232		}
1233		return &window
1234	}

1261	// ListHarnesses returns metadata for every registered harness.
1262	func (s *service) ListHarnesses(ctx context.Context) ([]HarnessInfo, error) {
1263		s.ensurePrimaryQuotaRefresh(ctx, quotaRefreshAsync)
1264		statuses := s.registry.Discover()
1265	
1266		// Index statuses by name for O(1) lookup.
1267		statusByName := make(map[string]harnesses.HarnessStatus, len(statuses))
1268		for _, st := range statuses {
1269			statusByName[st.Name] = st
1270		}
1271	
1272		// Emit in registry preference order.
1273		names := s.registry.Names()
1274		out := make([]HarnessInfo, 0, len(names))
1275	
1276		for _, name := range names {
1277			cfg, ok := s.registry.Get(name)
1278			if !ok {
1279				continue
1280			}
1281			st := statusByName[name]
1282	
1283			info := HarnessInfo{
1284				Name:                 name,
1285				Type:                 harnessType(cfg),
1286				Available:            st.Available,
1287				Path:                 st.Path,
1288				Error:                st.Error,
1289				Billing:              harnessPaymentKind(name, cfg),
1290				AutoRoutingEligible:  cfg.AutoRoutingEligible,
1291				TestOnly:             cfg.TestOnly,
1292				ExactPinSupport:      cfg.ExactPinSupport,
1293				DefaultModel:         cfg.DefaultModel,
1294				SupportedPermissions: supportedPermissions(cfg),
1295				SupportedReasoning:   supportedReasoning(cfg),
1296				CostClass:            cfg.CostClass,
1297				CapabilityMatrix:     harnessCapabilityMatrix(name, cfg),
1298			}
1299			if !st.Available {
1300				info.LastError = statusError(st.Error, "harness discovery", time.Now())
1301			}
1302	
1303			// Populate live Quota for harnesses that have durable quota caches.
1304			switch name {
1305			case "claude":
1306				info.Quota = claudeQuotaState()
1307				info.Account = claudeAccountStatus()
1308			case "codex":
1309				info.Quota = codexQuotaState()
1310				info.Account = codexAccountStatus()
1311				info.UsageWindows = s.codexUsageWindows()
1312			case "gemini":
1313				info.Quota = geminiQuotaState()
1314				info.Account = geminiAccountStatus()
1315				info.UsageWindows = s.geminiUsageWindows()
1316			}
1317	
1318			out = append(out, info)
1319		}
1320	
1321		return out, nil
1322	}

1	# Changelog
2	
3	All notable changes to Fizeau are recorded here.
4	Dates use the repo convention (`YYYY-MM-DD`); versions follow semver.
5	
6	## [Unreleased]
7	
8	### Breaking — v0.11 Routing Surface
9	
10	- Routing intent is now policy-based. Public callers use `--policy`,
11	  `--min-power`, and `--max-power`; hard pins remain `--model`, `--provider`,
12	  and `--harness`.
13	- Removed routing flags and service fields for the old route-reference surface:
14	  `--profile`, `--model-ref`, `ModelRef`, `ListProfiles`, `ResolveProfile`,
15	  and `ProfileAliases`.
16	- `fiz policies` and `fiz harnesses` expose the supported v0.11 inspection
17	  surfaces. Route-status JSON uses `policy` and `power_policy`.
18	- The canonical policy names are `cheap`, `default`, `smart`, and
19	  `air-gapped`. The dropped policy/alias names are: `fast`, `code-fast`,
20	  `code-economy`, `code-smart`, `standard`, `local`, `offline`, `code-high`,
21	  and `code-medium`.
22	- Pay-per-token providers are default-deny for automatic routing unless
23	  `include_by_default` opts them in. Explicit pins can still select excluded
24	  providers, but policy requirements such as `air-gapped` / `no_remote` still
25	  win.
26	
27	## [v0.10.9] — 2026-05-06
28	
29	### Changed
30	
31	- Sticky utilization routing now records and replays the evidence used to
32	  choose a destination, including endpoint load and route-health state, so the
33	  public routing projections can explain why a sticky route won.
34	- In-process sticky route leases now back the routing behavior for local model
35	  variants, with provider utilization probes normalized across `llama-server`,
36	  `vllm`, `rapid-mlx`, and `omlx`.
37	- Provider and route-status surfaces now carry the sticky-utilization evidence
38	  needed by the service facade and session replay helpers.
39	
40	### Tests
41	
42	- Coverage was added or refreshed for sticky route selection, route-health
43	  utilization storage, provider utilization probes, and cassette-based
44	  utilization recordings for the local server providers.
45	
46	## [v0.10.8] — 2026-05-05
47	
48	### Changed
49	
50	- Root `package fizeau` is now the public service facade for the cleanup
51	  covered by ADR-008. Concrete execution dispatch, transcript/progress
52	  rendering, route-health feedback, provider quota state, burn-rate tracking,
53	  and routing-quality aggregation now live behind internal package boundaries.
54	- Fizeau owns the public transcript/progress semantics for native and
55	  subprocess harness paths, so downstream consumers can consume service events
56	  and projections without parsing harness-native streams or private session-log
57	  records.
58	- Pure no-op compaction remains silent at the public service boundary; stale
59	  downstream no-op compaction stall policy should migrate away from that signal.
60	
61	### Tests
62	
63	- Public facade smoke coverage now exercises constructor/service import,
64	  progress events, route feedback/status, usage-window validation,
65	  provider quota wrappers, burn-rate wrappers, and routing-quality metric
66	  structs from `package fizeau_test`.
67	
68	## [v0.10.7] — 2026-05-04
69	
70	### Fixed
71	
72	- Routing now excludes fresh exhausted subscription harnesses before power
73	  checks, while still allowing unknown-power models when no power bound is
74	  requested.
75	- Claude quota status is normalized through the shared subscription quota view.
76	- Progress events now use compact one-line status text with task and round
77	  identity for native, subprocess, virtual, and script harness paths.
78	
79	## [v0.10.0] — 2026-05-02
80	
81	### Added — CONTRACT-003 observability fields
82	
83	- `ServiceExecuteRequest` and `RouteRequest` gain `Role` and `CorrelationID`
84	  string fields. Both are observational: echoed into `routing_decision` and
85	  `final` event `Metadata` plus the session-log header, but never into
86	  `text_delta` event metadata. Day 1 they do **not** enter the routing
87	  precedence chain and never affect eligibility filtering.
88	  - `Role` is normalized to lowercased alphanumeric + hyphen, max 64 chars.
89	    Invalid values are rejected pre-dispatch with the typed
90	    `*RoleNormalizationError`.
91	  - `CorrelationID` is normalized to printable ASCII (no control chars or
92	    whitespace), max 256 chars. Invalid values are rejected pre-dispatch with
93	    the typed `*CorrelationIDNormalizationError`.
94	- `ServiceRoutingActual` gains `Power int` — the catalog-projected power of the
95	  actually-dispatched Model. Zero means unknown / no catalog entry. DDx callers
96	  read this to compute next-attempt `MinPower` without importing catalog code.
97	- `RouteDecision` gains `Power int` carrying the same value, populated by
98	  `ResolveRoute`.
99	- The reserved cross-tool metadata key list grows with `role` and
100	  `correlation_id`. When the caller sets both a top-level field and the same
101	  reserved metadata key, the top-level field wins and a
102	  `metadata_key_collision` warning is appended to the final event's
103	  `Warnings`.
104	
105	This is a strict additive change to the contract: existing callers that do not
106	set `Role` or `CorrelationID` see no behavior change. DDx and other downstream
107	consumers can import the new fields directly from the tagged release.
108

112	
113	- `go install github.com/easel/fizeau/cmd/fiz@<version>` builds now
114	  report the module version from Go build metadata when release ldflags are not

1	// Package registry is the single source of truth for provider-type →
2	// factory mappings.
3	//
4	// Why this package exists. Before it, the agent had two parallel
5	// factories that knew about provider types: internal/config/config.go
6	// `BuildProvider` (config-time) and service_native_provider.go
7	// `buildNativeProvider` (service-execute time). When lucebox + vllm
8	// were added in v0.9.18-.20, only the first factory got the new types;
9	// the second one rejected requests pinning those providers at execute
10	// time. Two factories drifting is the architectural smell `agent-8e4eb44c`
11	// was filed to fix.
12	//
13	// Each provider package registers a Descriptor in its init(). Both
14	// factories call Lookup. New providers are one Register call away from
15	// being usable through every code path.
16	//
17	// The registry deliberately covers only the factory drift case in v1.
18	// Other type-switch sites (default port, base URL, surface map,
19	// capability flags, validators) are local to internal/config and
20	// don't have the cross-package drift problem; they stay there. Future
21	// drift cases can be additively folded into the registry as they appear.
22	package registry
23	
24	import (
25		"sort"
26		"sync"
27	
28		agentcore "github.com/easel/fizeau/internal/core"
29		"github.com/easel/fizeau/internal/provider/quotaheaders"
30		"github.com/easel/fizeau/internal/reasoning"
31	)
32	
33	// Inputs is the canonical factory input shape. Both the config-time
34	// path (internal/config builds this from a ProviderConfig) and the
35	// service-time path (service_native_provider builds this from a
36	// ServiceProviderEntry) populate it. Provider factories take exactly
37	// one argument so the call sites can't accidentally diverge.
38	type Inputs struct {
39		// ProviderName is the user-facing config name for this provider
40		// (e.g., "vidar-omlx"). Distinct from the provider TYPE (e.g.,
41		// "omlx"). Some factories include it in attribution metadata.
42		ProviderName string
43	
44		// BaseURL is the resolved endpoint (with default substituted).
45		BaseURL string
46		// APIKey is the bearer token; empty for unauthenticated endpoints.
47		APIKey string
48		// Model is the configured default; empty when discovery is in use.
49		Model string
50	
51		// ModelPattern is an optional regex used by openai-compat providers
52		// during model discovery (Tier-2 ranking).
53		ModelPattern string
54	
55		// KnownModels maps surface model IDs to canonical IDs from the
56		// model catalog. Used by openai-compat providers to recognize
57		// catalog-tracked models on a discovery list.
58		KnownModels map[string]string
59	
60		// Headers is extra HTTP headers (OpenRouter, Azure, custom).
61		Headers map[string]string
62	
63		// Reasoning is the per-provider default reasoning policy.
64		Reasoning reasoning.Reasoning
65	
66		// ModelReasoningWire is a model_id → reasoning_wire map sourced
67		// from the catalog. Today only openrouter and openai-compat
68		// providers consume it; others ignore it.
69		ModelReasoningWire map[string]string
70	
71		// QuotaSignalObserver, when set, is forwarded to provider factories
72		// that support per-response rate-limit-header parsing (anthropic,
73		// openai, openrouter). The service layer wires this to the provider
74		// quota state machine so dispatch can route around providers whose
75		// subscription/daily cap is hit. Providers that do not parse rate-limit
76		// headers ignore this field.
77		QuotaSignalObserver func(quotaheaders.Signal)
78	}
79	
80	// Factory builds a provider from canonical inputs.
81	type Factory func(Inputs) agentcore.Provider
82	
83	// Descriptor pairs a provider type with its factory + a small set of
84	// invariants. Provider packages register these in init().
85	type Descriptor struct {
86		// Type is the canonical provider-type string (e.g., "omlx").
87		// Matches the `type:` value in user config and the descriptor's
88		// case in internal/config switches.
89		Type string
90	
91		// Factory builds an agent.Provider from canonical inputs.
92		Factory Factory
93	
94		// DefaultBaseURL is used by the config-normalizer when no base_url
95		// is explicitly set.
96		DefaultBaseURL string
97	
98		// DefaultPort is used by endpoint-config inference when only a
99		// host is supplied. 0 means "no inference; require explicit BaseURL".
100		DefaultPort int
101	
102		// IntrospectionFn, when non-nil, is the L1 live-introspection adapter
103		// for this provider type. Register automatically wires it into the
104		// introspection adapter map so IntrospectProvider can invoke it.
105		IntrospectionFn IntrospectionFn
106	}
107	
108	var (
109		mu      sync.RWMutex
110		entries = map[string]Descriptor{}
111	)
112	
113	// Register installs a provider Descriptor. Called from each provider
114	// package's init() function. Re-registering the same type panics —
115	// duplicate registration is always a programmer error.
116	func Register(d Descriptor) {
117		if d.Type == "" {
118			panic("provider/registry: Descriptor.Type is required")
119		}
120		if d.Factory == nil {
121			panic("provider/registry: Descriptor.Factory is required for type " + d.Type)
122		}
123		mu.Lock()
124		defer mu.Unlock()
125		if _, exists := entries[d.Type]; exists {
126			panic("provider/registry: duplicate registration for type " + d.Type)
127		}
128		entries[d.Type] = d
129		if d.IntrospectionFn != nil {
130			RegisterIntrospectionAdapter(d.Type, d.IntrospectionFn)
131		}
132	}
133	
134	// Lookup returns the Descriptor for a provider type, or false if the
135	// type is not registered.
136	func Lookup(typ string) (Descriptor, bool) {
137		mu.RLock()
138		defer mu.RUnlock()
139		d, ok := entries[typ]
140		return d, ok
141	}
142	
143	// Types returns all registered provider type names in sorted order.
144	// Used by validators and conformance descriptors that iterate over
145	// every known provider type.
146	func Types() []string {
147		mu.RLock()
148		defer mu.RUnlock()
149		out := make([]string, 0, len(entries))
150		for t := range entries {
151			out = append(out, t)
152		}
153		sort.Strings(out)
154		return out
155	}

8	// overflowPattern matches error messages that indicate the request exceeded the
9	// model's context window.
10	var overflowPattern = regexp.MustCompile(
11		`(?i)context.?length|token.?limit|maximum.?context|context.?window|` +
12			`too.?long|exceeds.*limit|reduce.*length|reduce.*message`,
13	)
14	
15	// IsContextOverflowError reports whether err indicates the request exceeded
16	// the model's context window.
17	func IsContextOverflowError(err error) bool {
18		if err == nil {
19			return false
20		}
21		return overflowPattern.MatchString(err.Error())
22	}
23	
24	// transientPattern matches error messages that indicate a transient condition
25	// (network blips, rate limits, server overload) that is safe to retry.
26	var transientPattern = regexp.MustCompile(
27		`(?i)overloaded|rate.?limit|too many requests|\b(?:429|500|502|503|504)\b|` +
28			`service.?unavailable|server.?error|internal.?error|` +
29			`network.?error|connection.?error|connection.?refused|` +
30			`other side closed|fetch failed|socket hang.?up|` +
31			`ended without|timed?.?out|timeout|i/o timeout|` +
32			`EOF|connection reset|broken pipe`,
33	)
34	
35	// fatalPattern matches error messages that indicate a permanent failure
36	// (authentication/authorization). These should never be retried even if they
37	// also match a transient pattern.
38	var fatalPattern = regexp.MustCompile(
39		`(?i)\b(?:401|403)\b|unauthorized|forbidden|invalid.?api.?key|authentication`,
40	)
41	
42	// IsTransientError reports whether err is a transient provider error
43	// that is safe to retry (network issues, rate limits, server overload).
44	// Returns false for fatal errors (auth failures, bad requests, etc.).
45	func IsTransientError(err error) bool {
46		if err == nil {
47			return false
48		}
49		// Provider-capability errors are deterministic for a given provider+model
50		// combination. Retrying just burns the same call repeatedly with the same
51		// outcome, so classify them as non-transient regardless of substring shape.
52		if errors.Is(err, ErrProviderCapabilityMissing) {
53			return false
54		}
55		msg := err.Error()
56		if fatalPattern.MatchString(msg) {
57			return false
58		}
59		return transientPattern.MatchString(msg)
60	}

**Key files:**

**`provider_error.go`:**
- `IsContextOverflowError(err)` - Detects context window overflow
  - Pattern: matches "context", "token", "limit", "window", "too long", etc.
- `IsTransientError(err)` - Detects transient errors safe to retry
  - Pattern: matches "overloaded", "rate limit", "503", "timeout", "EOF", "connection reset", etc.
  - Returns false for fatal errors (401, 403, "unauthorized", auth failures)
  - Special case: `ErrProviderCapabilityMissing` returns false (deterministic)
- `overflowPattern`, `transientPattern`, `fatalPattern` - Regex patterns for error classification

**`loop.go`:**
- `Run(ctx, req Request)` - Main agent loop (line 24)
  - **Retry logic (lines 648-661):**
    ```
    const maxProviderAttempts = 5
    if !IsTransientError(err):
        return error (fatal)
    if attempt < maxProviderAttempts:
        delaySeconds = 1 << min(attempt-1, 10)  // exponential backoff: 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s
        sleep(delaySeconds)
        continue  // retry
    else:
        return error (exhausted retries)
    ```
  - Emits telemetry/events for each attempt
  - Handles context overflow with compaction retry
  - Cost tracking per attempt

---

### How These Components Interact

**Request Flow:**

1. **Service Entry** (`Execute()` in root `service.go`)
   - Receives `ServiceExecuteRequest` with optional model/provider/policy pins
   
2. **Routing** (`ResolveRoute()` in `service_routing.go`)
   - Calls `internal/routing.Resolve()` with request
   - Uses `routehealth.Store` for failure history
   - Uses `routehealth.LeaseStore` for sticky assignments
   - Uses `routehealth.UtilizationStore` for load balancing
   - Returns `RouteDecision` with ranked candidates

3. **Dispatch** (`serviceimpl.DispatchExecuteRun()`)
   - Routes to runner based on harness type:
     - "fiz" or "" → Native loop (`RunNative`)
     - "claude"/"codex"/"gemini" → Subprocess harnesses
     - "virtual" → Virtual provider
     - "script" → Script execution

4. **Native Loop** (`internal/core.Run()`)
   - Executes prompt against resolved provider
   - **Retry on transient provider errors:**
     - Up to 5 attempts per iteration
     - Exponential backoff: 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s
     - Non-transient errors fail immediately
     - Transient errors (rate limits, 503, timeout, etc.) retry
   - Records result to `routehealth.Store` via `RecordRouteAttempt()`
   - Handles tool calling loop until completion

5. **Health Feedback** (`RecordRouteAttempt()`)
   - Updates `routehealth.Store` with attempt status
   - Success clears failures; failure records cooldown
   - Metrics updated for routing signals

```go
type RouteRequest struct {
    Policy                string         // policy bundle: cheap|default|smart|air-gapped
    Model                 string         // exact model pin
    Provider              string         // exact provider pin
    Harness               string         // harness preference
    Reasoning             Reasoning      // reasoning level
    Permissions           string         // permission level
    AllowLocal            bool           // local endpoint flag
    Require               []string       // policy requirements
    MinPower, MaxPower    int            // power bounds
    EstimatedPromptTokens int            // for context gating
    RequiresTools         bool           // tool support gate
    CachePolicy           string         // "default" | "off"
    Role                  string         // observational tag
    CorrelationID         string         // observational correlation ID
}
```

**RouteDecision** (service.go lines 427-465):
```go
type RouteDecision struct {
    RequestedPolicy        string
    SnapshotCapturedAt     time.Time
    PowerPolicy            RoutePowerPolicy
    Harness, Provider      string
    Endpoint, ServerInstance string
    Model                  string
    Reason                 string
    Sticky                 RouteStickyState
    Utilization            RouteUtilizationState
    Power                  int
    Candidates             []RouteCandidate  // full ranked list
}
```

**routehealth.Store** (routehealth/store.go):
- Tracks per-attempt feedback (success/failure)
- Maintains failure cooldown records
- Computes success-rate and latency metrics for routing

**Retry Behavior** (internal/core/loop.go lines 648-661):
- **Max attempts:** 5 per iteration
- **Backoff:** Exponential (1, 2, 4, 8, 16 seconds)
- **Triggers:** Transient errors only (rate limits, 503, timeout, EOF, connection errors)
- **Excluded:** Fatal errors (401/403 auth, bad requests)
- **Special:** Context overflow can trigger compaction retry

---

### CHANGELOG.md Summary (Key Versions)

**v0.11 (Unreleased):**
- Breaking: Routing is now policy-based (--policy, --min-power, --max-power)
- Pay-per-token providers default to excluded unless include_by_default set

**v0.10.9 (2026-05-06):**
- Sticky utilization routing with lease store
- Provider utilization probes normalized across local providers
- Route-health state integration

**v0.10.8 (2026-05-05):**
- Root package fizeau is public service facade
- Internal dispatch, routing, quota state behind boundaries
- Pure no-op compaction remains silent

**v0.10.7 (2026-05-04):**
- Routing excludes exhausted subscriptions before power checks
- Claude quota through shared subscription view
- Compact progress events

**v0.10.0 (2026-05-02):**
- Added Role and CorrelationID fields (observational)
- RouteDecision.Power for next-attempt planning
- Reserved metadata keys

**v0.9.26 (2026-05-01):**
- Renamed from ddx-agent/agent to Fizeau/fiz (breaking)

---

### Architecture Summary

The Fizeau routing and execution system implements:

1. **Multi-layer Routing:**
   - Policy-based (cheap/default/smart/air-gapped)
   - Power-hint optimization (1-10 scale)
   - Provider/model explicit pins
   - Sticky affinity for continuity

2. **Provider Abstraction:**
   - Factory registry (`internal/provider/registry`)
   - OpenAI-compatible base implementation
   - Provider-specific adaptations (quota, reasoning, cost)
   - Live health and utilization tracking

3. **Resilient Execution:**
   - Up to 5 transient-error retries per provider call
   - Exponential backoff (1-16 seconds)
   - Non-transient errors fail immediately
   - Context overflow triggers compaction + retry

4. **In-Process State Management:**
   - Failure cooldown store (30s default TTL)
   - Sticky lease store (5m default TTL)
   - Utilization sample store (fresh/stale tracking)
   - Routing quality metrics aggregation

This architecture enables transparent failover, cost optimization, and observability without external coordination.

641						result.Error = fmt.Errorf("agent: provider error: %w", err)
642						result.Duration = time.Since(start)
643						snapshotMessages()
644						emitFinalSessionEnd(req.Callback, sessionID, &seq, req.Provider, &result, req.Metadata)
645						return result, result.Error
646					}
647	
648					if attempt < maxProviderAttempts {
649						delaySeconds := time.Duration(1 << min(attempt-1, 10))
650						delay := time.Second * delaySeconds
651						slog.Warn("provider error, retrying", "attempt", attempt, "err", err, "delay", delay)
652						select {
653						case <-ctx.Done():
654							result.Status = StatusCancelled
655							result.Duration = time.Since(start)
656							snapshotMessages()
657							emitFinalSessionEnd(req.Callback, sessionID, &seq, req.Provider, &result, req.Metadata)
658							return result, nil
659						case <-time.After(delay):
660						}
661						continue
662					}
663	
664					result.Status = StatusError
665					result.Error = fmt.Errorf("agent: provider error: %w", err)
666					result.Duration = time.Since(start)
667					snapshotMessages()
668					emitFinalSessionEnd(req.Callback, sessionID, &seq, req.Provider, &result, req.Metadata)
669					return result, result.Error
670				}
671	
672				// Emit LLM response event with full tool call bodies.
673				responseData := map[string]any{
674					"attempt_index":     attempt,
675					"content":           resp.Content,
676					"tool_calls":        resp.ToolCalls,
677					"usage":             resp.Usage,
678					"latency_ms":        llmDuration.Milliseconds(),
679					"model":             resp.Model,
680					"finish_reason":     resp.FinishReason,
681					"attempt":           resp.Attempt,
682					"reasoning_intent":  req.Reasoning,
683					"reasoning_emitted": result.ReasoningEmitted,
684				}
685				if costKnown {
686					responseData["cost_usd"] = iterCost
687				}
688				emitCallback(req.Callback, Event{
689					SessionID: sessionID,
690					Seq:       seq,
691					Type:      EventLLMResponse,
692					Timestamp: time.Now().UTC(),
693					Data:      mustMarshal(responseData),
694				})
695				seq++
696				annotateChatSpan(chatSpan, resp)
697				chatSpan.End()
698

1	package core
2	
3	import (
4		"errors"
5		"regexp"
6	)
7	
8	// overflowPattern matches error messages that indicate the request exceeded the
9	// model's context window.
10	var overflowPattern = regexp.MustCompile(
11		`(?i)context.?length|token.?limit|maximum.?context|context.?window|` +
12			`too.?long|exceeds.*limit|reduce.*length|reduce.*message`,
13	)
14	
15	// IsContextOverflowError reports whether err indicates the request exceeded
16	// the model's context window.
17	func IsContextOverflowError(err error) bool {
18		if err == nil {
19			return false
20		}
21		return overflowPattern.MatchString(err.Error())
22	}
23	
24	// transientPattern matches error messages that indicate a transient condition
25	// (network blips, rate limits, server overload) that is safe to retry.
26	var transientPattern = regexp.MustCompile(
27		`(?i)overloaded|rate.?limit|too many requests|\b(?:429|500|502|503|504)\b|` +
28			`service.?unavailable|server.?error|internal.?error|` +
29			`network.?error|connection.?error|connection.?refused|` +
30			`other side closed|fetch failed|socket hang.?up|` +
31			`ended without|timed?.?out|timeout|i/o timeout|` +
32			`EOF|connection reset|broken pipe`,
33	)
34	
35	// fatalPattern matches error messages that indicate a permanent failure
36	// (authentication/authorization). These should never be retried even if they
37	// also match a transient pattern.
38	var fatalPattern = regexp.MustCompile(
39		`(?i)\b(?:401|403)\b|unauthorized|forbidden|invalid.?api.?key|authentication`,
40	)
41	
42	// IsTransientError reports whether err is a transient provider error
43	// that is safe to retry (network issues, rate limits, server overload).
44	// Returns false for fatal errors (auth failures, bad requests, etc.).
45	func IsTransientError(err error) bool {
46		if err == nil {
47			return false
48		}
49		// Provider-capability errors are deterministic for a given provider+model
50		// combination. Retrying just burns the same call repeatedly with the same
51		// outcome, so classify them as non-transient regardless of substring shape.
52		if errors.Is(err, ErrProviderCapabilityMissing) {
53			return false
54		}
55		msg := err.Error()
56		if fatalPattern.MatchString(msg) {
57			return false
58		}
59		return transientPattern.MatchString(msg)
60	}

52	// MinContextWindow returns the minimum context window the request requires,
53	// derived from EstimatedPromptTokens with a safety margin.
54	func (r Request) MinContextWindow() int {
55		if r.EstimatedPromptTokens <= 0 {

59		return r.EstimatedPromptTokens + r.EstimatedPromptTokens/4

475	// Resolve runs the engine end-to-end and returns a Decision.
476	//
477	// The engine:
478	//  1. Enumerates (harness, provider, model) candidates from inputs.
479	//  2. Applies gating (capability, override, model-pin, surface).
480	//  3. Scores per policy with cooldown demotion + perf bias.
481	//  4. Sorts viable → score → cost → locality → name.
482	//  5. Returns the top viable candidate with the full ranked list.
483	//
484	// Returns an error only when no viable candidate exists.
485	func Resolve(req Request, in Inputs) (*Decision, error) {
486		if in.Now.IsZero() {
487			in.Now = time.Now()
488		}
489		policyName, err := canonicalPolicy(req.Policy)
490		if err != nil {
491			return &Decision{}, err
492		}
493		req.Policy = policyName
494		if err := validateRequirements(req); err != nil {
495			return &Decision{}, err
496		}
497	
498		canonicalHarness := req.Harness
499		if canonicalHarness == "local" {
500			canonicalHarness = "fiz"
501		}
502		if err := explicitPinError(req, in); err != nil {
503			return &Decision{}, err
504		}
505	
506		var ranked []rankedCandidate
507		for _, h := range in.Harnesses {
508			// TestOnly harnesses (script/virtual) only reachable via explicit override.
509			if h.TestOnly && canonicalHarness != h.Name {
510				continue
511			}
512			// Automatic profile/tier routing is restricted to harnesses with full
513			// coverage. Explicit Harness pins can still use experimental/ad-hoc
514			// harnesses.
515			if canonicalHarness == "" && !h.AutoRoutingEligible {
516				continue
517			}
518			// Hard harness override: skip non-matching harnesses.
519			if canonicalHarness != "" && canonicalHarness != h.Name {
520				continue
521			}
522			entries := buildHarnessCandidates(h, req, in)
523			ranked = append(ranked, entries...)
524		}
525	
526		// Compute scores only after eligibility is final. Rejected candidates keep
527		// a zero score because cost/utilization/performance/sticky ranking should
528		// never influence the eligibility boundary.
529		for i := range ranked {
530			if !ranked[i].out.Eligible {
531				continue
532			}
533			ranked[i].out.Score = scorePolicy(req.Policy, ranked[i].internal)
534			ranked[i].out.ScoreComponents = scoreComponents(req.Policy, ranked[i].internal)
535			ranked[i].out.Reason = fmt.Sprintf("policy=%s; score=%.1f", req.Policy, ranked[i].out.Score)
536		}
537		neutralCost, hasKnownCost := neutralKnownCost(ranked)
538	
539		// Sort: eligible first, then descending score, then cost, then locality,
540		// then alphabetical.
541		sort.SliceStable(ranked, func(i, j int) bool {
542			ei, ej := ranked[i].out.Eligible, ranked[j].out.Eligible
543			if ei != ej {
544				return ei
545			}
546			if !ei {
547				return ranked[i].out.Harness < ranked[j].out.Harness
548			}
549			if ranked[i].out.Score != ranked[j].out.Score {
550				return ranked[i].out.Score > ranked[j].out.Score
551			}
552			if hasKnownCost {
553				ci := candidateCostTieValue(ranked[i], neutralCost)
554				cj := candidateCostTieValue(ranked[j], neutralCost)
555				if ci != cj {
556					return ci < cj
557				}
558			}
559			// Locality tiebreak: prefer local cost-class.
560			li := costClassRank[ranked[i].internal.CostClass] == 0
561			lj := costClassRank[ranked[j].internal.CostClass] == 0
562			if li != lj {
563				return li
564			}
565			if sameLocalEndpointGroup(ranked[i].internal, ranked[j].internal) {
566				if ranked[i].internal.EndpointSaturated != ranked[j].internal.EndpointSaturated {
567					return !ranked[i].internal.EndpointSaturated
568				}
569				if ranked[i].internal.EndpointLoad != ranked[j].internal.EndpointLoad {
570					return ranked[i].internal.EndpointLoad < ranked[j].internal.EndpointLoad
571				}
572			}
573			if ranked[i].out.Harness != ranked[j].out.Harness {
574				return ranked[i].out.Harness < ranked[j].out.Harness
575			}
576			return ranked[i].out.Provider < ranked[j].out.Provider
577		})
578	
579		out := make([]Candidate, len(ranked))
580		for i := range ranked {
581			out[i] = ranked[i].out
582		}
583	
584		for i := range out {
585			if out[i].Eligible {
586				return &Decision{
587					Harness:        out[i].Harness,
588					Provider:       out[i].Provider,
589					Endpoint:       out[i].Endpoint,
590					ServerInstance: out[i].ServerInstance,
591					Model:          out[i].Model,
592					Reason:         fmt.Sprintf("policy=%s; score=%.1f", req.Policy, out[i].Score),
593					Candidates:     out,
594				}, nil
595			}
596		}
597		if quotaErr := allProvidersQuotaExhaustedError(ranked); quotaErr != nil {
598			return &Decision{Candidates: out}, quotaErr
599		}
600		if requested := requestedModelIntent(req); requested != "" && req.Provider == "" && canonicalHarness == "" && hasLiveDiscoveryCandidates(ranked) {
601			return &Decision{Candidates: out}, fmt.Errorf("no live endpoint offers a match for %s", requested)
602		}
603		if missingRequirement := missingPolicyRequirement(req, out); missingRequirement != "" {
604			return &Decision{Candidates: out}, &ErrPolicyRequirementUnsatisfied{
605				Policy:      req.Policy,
606				Requirement: missingRequirement,
607				Rejected:    len(out),
608			}
609		}
610		return &Decision{Candidates: out}, noViableCandidateError(req, len(out))
611	}
612	
613	// allProvidersQuotaExhaustedError returns ErrAllProvidersQuotaExhausted when
614	// at least one routing candidate was rejected solely because its provider is
615	// in the quota_exhausted state and no other candidate was eligible. The set
616	// of otherwise-eligible candidates collapses to those flagged with
617	// quotaExhaustedRetryAfter (the engine sets this only when the candidate
618	// passed every other gate). When that set is empty the existing
619	// no-viable / no-profile-candidate / live-discovery errors describe the
620	// failure more precisely.
621	func allProvidersQuotaExhaustedError(ranked []rankedCandidate) error {
622		var exhausted []string
623		seen := make(map[string]struct{})
624		var earliest time.Time
625		for _, c := range ranked {
626			if c.quotaExhaustedRetryAfter.IsZero() {
627				continue
628			}
629			name := c.out.Provider
630			if name == "" {
631				name = c.out.Harness
632			}
633			if _, dup := seen[name]; !dup {
634				seen[name] = struct{}{}
635				exhausted = append(exhausted, name)
636			}
637			if earliest.IsZero() || c.quotaExhaustedRetryAfter.Before(earliest) {
638				earliest = c.quotaExhaustedRetryAfter
639			}
640		}
641		if len(exhausted) == 0 {
642			return nil
643		}
644		return &ErrAllProvidersQuotaExhausted{
645			RetryAfter:         earliest,
646			ExhaustedProviders: exhausted,
647		}
648	}

686	func explicitPinError(req Request, in Inputs) error {
687		canonicalHarness := canonicalHarnessPin(req.Harness)
688		if requirement, attemptedPin := requirementPinConflict(req, in, canonicalHarness); requirement != "" {
689			return &ErrPolicyRequirementUnsatisfied{
690				Policy:       req.Policy,
691				Requirement:  requirement,
692				AttemptedPin: attemptedPin,
693			}
694		}
695	
696		if canonicalHarness != "" && req.Provider != "" && !harnessCanReachProvider(in.Harnesses, canonicalHarness, req.Provider) {
697			return &ErrUnsatisfiablePin{
698				Pin:    "harness=" + canonicalHarness + "+provider=" + req.Provider,
699				Reason: "provider is not reachable through harness",
700			}
701		}
702		if canonicalHarness != "" && req.Model != "" {
703			// Provider-routed subprocess harnesses can route any provider-pinned
704			// model (lmstudio, omlx, openrouter, etc.); the harness CLI owns
705			// concrete model validation in that case.
706			if providerRoutedHarnessAcceptsProviderPinnedModel(canonicalHarness) && req.Provider != "" {
707				return nil
708			}
709			h, ok := findHarness(in.Harnesses, canonicalHarness)
710			if ok && h.SupportedModels != nil && !harnessSupportsModel(h.SupportedModels, req.Model) {
711				return &ErrUnsatisfiablePin{
712					Pin:    "harness=" + canonicalHarness + "+model=" + req.Model,
713					Reason: "model is not supported by harness",
714				}
715			}
716			if ok && h.SupportedModels == nil && !harnessCanServeExactModel(h, req.Model) {
717				return &ErrUnsatisfiablePin{
718					Pin:    "harness=" + canonicalHarness + "+model=" + req.Model,
719					Reason: "model is not supported by harness",
720				}
721			}
722		}
723		if req.Provider != "" && req.Model != "" && !providerCanServeModel(in.Harnesses, canonicalHarness, req.Provider, req.Model) {
724			return &ErrUnsatisfiablePin{
725				Pin:    "provider=" + req.Provider + "+model=" + req.Model,
726				Reason: "model is not available on provider",
727			}
728		}
729		return nil
730	}

804	func providerCanServeModel(harnesses []HarnessEntry, canonicalHarness, provider, model string) bool {
805		for _, h := range harnesses {
806			if canonicalHarness != "" && h.Name != canonicalHarness {
807				continue
808			}
809			providers := h.Providers
810			if len(providers) == 0 {
811				providers = []ProviderEntry{{Name: ""}}
812			}
813			for _, p := range providers {
814				if candidateProviderIdentity(h, p) != provider {
815					continue
816				}
817				if len(p.DiscoveredIDs) > 0 {
818					return FuzzyMatch(model, p.DiscoveredIDs) != ""
819				}
820				if p.DiscoveryAttempted {
821					return false
822				}
823				return p.DefaultModel == "" || p.DefaultModel == model || harnessCanServeExactModel(h, model)
824			}
825		}
826		return false
827	}

862	func missingPolicyRequirement(req Request, candidates []Candidate) string {
863		if req.Policy == "" {
864			return ""
865		}
866		switch req.ProviderPreference {
867		case ProviderPreferenceLocalOnly:
868			return "local endpoint"
869		case ProviderPreferenceSubscriptionOnly:
870			return "subscription harness"
871		}
872		if requiresNoRemote(req) {
873			if anyLocalCandidate(candidates) && !anyEligibleCandidate(candidates) {
874				return "no_remote"
875			}
876			if allCandidatesRejectedFor(candidates, FilterReasonPolicyFiltered) {
877				return "no_remote"
878			}
879		}
880		if allCandidatesRejectedFor(candidates, FilterReasonMeteredOptInRequired) {
881			return "metered opt-in"
882		}
883		if !requestAllowsLocal(req) && allCandidatesRejectedFor(candidates, FilterReasonPolicyFiltered) {
884			return "allow_local=false"
885		}
886		return ""
887	}

942	type rankedCandidate struct {

944		internal candidateInternal
945		// quotaExhaustedRetryAfter is non-zero when this candidate would have been
946		// eligible save for its provider being in the quota_exhausted state.
947		// Resolve uses this to detect the "all eligible providers quota-exhausted"
948		// case and surface ErrAllProvidersQuotaExhausted.

952	// buildHarnessCandidates expands one HarnessEntry into 1..N candidates, one
953	// per (harness, provider, resolved-model) tuple.
954	func buildHarnessCandidates(h HarnessEntry, req Request, in Inputs) []rankedCandidate {
955		caps := Capabilities{
956			SupportsTools:      h.SupportsTools,
957			SupportedReasoning: h.SupportedReasoning,
958			MaxReasoningTokens: h.MaxReasoningTokens,
959			SupportedPerms:     h.SupportedPerms,
960			ExactPinSupport:    h.ExactPinSupport,
961			SupportedModels:    h.SupportedModels,
962		}
963	
964		if !h.Available {
965			return []rankedCandidate{{
966				out: Candidate{
967					Harness:      h.Name,
968					CostSource:   CostSourceUnknown,
969					Reason:       "harness not available",
970					FilterReason: FilterReasonUnhealthy,
971				},
972				internal: candidateInternal{Harness: h.Name, CostClass: h.CostClass, CostSource: CostSourceUnknown},
973			}}
974		}
975	
976		histRate, hasHist := in.HistoricalSuccess[h.Name]
977		if !hasHist {
978			histRate = -1
979		}
980	
981		// Enumerate providers. For harnesses with no providers (subprocess harnesses
982		// with vendor-managed billing), emit a single virtual provider entry.
983		providers := h.Providers
984		if len(providers) == 0 {
985			providers = []ProviderEntry{{Name: ""}}
986		}
987	
988		out := make([]rankedCandidate, 0, len(providers))
989		minCtx := req.MinContextWindow()
990		stickyServerInstance := ""
991		if req.CorrelationID != "" && in.StickyServerInstanceResolver != nil {
992			stickyServerInstance, _ = in.StickyServerInstanceResolver(req.CorrelationID)
993		}
994		for _, p := range providers {
995			model, reason := resolveModel(h, p, req, in)
996			ctxWin := 0
997			ctxSrc := ContextSourceUnknown
998			if p.ContextWindows != nil {
999				if v, ok := p.ContextWindows[model]; ok {
1000					ctxWin = v
1001				}
1002			}
1003			if ctxWin > 0 && p.ContextWindowSources != nil {
1004				if src, ok := p.ContextWindowSources[model]; ok && src != "" {
1005					ctxSrc = src
1006				}
1007			}
1008			if ctxWin == 0 && p.ContextWindow > 0 {
1009				ctxWin = p.ContextWindow
1010				if p.ContextWindowSource != "" {
1011					ctxSrc = p.ContextWindowSource
1012				}
1013			}
1014			if ctxWin == 0 && model != "" {
1015				ctxWin = 131072
1016				ctxSrc = ContextSourceDefault
1017			}
1018			if model == "" {
1019				ctxSrc = ContextSourceUnknown
1020			}
1021			entryCaps := caps
1022			entryCaps.ContextWindow = ctxWin
1023			entryCaps.SupportsTools = caps.SupportsTools || p.SupportsTools
1024			if len(p.DiscoveredIDs) > 0 {
1025				entryCaps.SupportedModels = nil
1026			}
1027	
1028			key := ProviderModelKey(p.Name, p.EndpointName, model)
1029			obs := in.ObservedSpeedTPS[key]
1030			if obs == 0 && p.EndpointName != "" {
1031				obs = in.ObservedSpeedTPS[ProviderModelKey(p.Name, "", model)]
1032			}
1033			providerSuccessRate := -1.0
1034			if rate, ok := in.ProviderSuccessRate[key]; ok {
1035				providerSuccessRate = rate
1036			} else if p.EndpointName != "" {
1037				if rate, ok := in.ProviderSuccessRate[ProviderModelKey(p.Name, "", model)]; ok {
1038					providerSuccessRate = rate
1039				}
1040			}
1041			latencyMS := in.ObservedLatencyMS[key]
1042			if latencyMS == 0 && p.EndpointName != "" {
1043				latencyMS = in.ObservedLatencyMS[ProviderModelKey(p.Name, "", model)]
1044			}
1045			power := candidatePower(in.ModelEligibility, model)
1046			endpointLoad := EndpointLoad{}
1047			if in.EndpointLoadResolver != nil {
1048				loadProvider, loadEndpoint := candidateLoadIdentity(h, p)
1049				if resolved, ok := in.EndpointLoadResolver(loadProvider, loadEndpoint, model); ok {
1050					endpointLoad = resolved
1051				}
1052			} else if load, ok := in.EndpointLoads[key]; ok {
1053				endpointLoad = load
1054			}
1055	
1056			gateReq := resolveRequestReasoning(req, h.Surface, in.ReasoningResolver)
1057			billingKind := candidateBillingKind(h, p)
1058	
1059			eligible := true
1060			var filterReason FilterReason
1061			if reason == "" {
1062				// Subscription quota is a hard availability gate and must be
1063				// reported before catalog metadata gates. An exhausted Claude/Codex/
1064				// Gemini account is never a viable candidate, regardless of whether
1065				// the resolved model has complete power metadata.
1066				if h.IsSubscription && !h.SubscriptionOK {
1067					eligible = false
1068					reason = h.QuotaReason
1069					if reason == "" {
1070						if h.QuotaStale {
1071							reason = "subscription quota unavailable"
1072						} else {
1073							reason = "subscription quota exhausted"
1074						}
1075					}
1076					filterReason = FilterReasonUnhealthy
1077				} else if g, fr := CheckPowerEligibility(in.ModelEligibility, model, gateReq); g != "" {
1078					eligible = false
1079					reason = g
1080					filterReason = fr
1081				} else if g, fr := CheckGating(entryCaps, gateReq); g != "" {
1082					eligible = false
1083					reason = g
1084					filterReason = fr
1085				}
1086			} else {
1087				eligible = false
1088				// resolveModel rejection — model resolution is a capability
1089				// mismatch with no specific public category, so fall through
1090				// to the catch-all.
1091				filterReason = FilterReasonScoredBelowTop
1092			}
1093	
1094			// Hard preference filtering.
1095			if eligible {
1096				if !requestAllowsLocal(req) && candidateIsLocal(h, p) {
1097					eligible = false
1098					reason = "policy disallows local candidates"
1099					filterReason = FilterReasonPolicyFiltered
1100				}
1101			}
1102			if eligible && requiresNoRemote(req) && !candidateIsLocal(h, p) {
1103				eligible = false
1104				reason = "policy requires no_remote"
1105				filterReason = FilterReasonPolicyFiltered
1106			}
1107	
1108			if eligible {
1109				switch req.ProviderPreference {
1110				case ProviderPreferenceLocalOnly:
1111					if !h.IsLocal {
1112						eligible = false
1113						reason = "preference is local-only"
1114						filterReason = FilterReasonUnhealthy
1115					}
1116				case ProviderPreferenceSubscriptionOnly:
1117					if !h.IsSubscription {
1118						eligible = false
1119						reason = "preference is subscription-only"
1120						filterReason = FilterReasonUnhealthy
1121					}
1122				}
1123			}
1124	
1125			if eligible {
1126				if g, fr := CheckProviderDefaultEligibility(candidateProviderIdentity(h, p), p.ActualCashSpend, billingKind, p.ExcludeFromDefaultRouting, req); g != "" {
1127					eligible = false
1128					reason = g
1129					filterReason = fr
1130				}
1131			}
1132	
1133			if eligible && req.Provider != "" && req.Provider != candidateProviderIdentity(h, p) {
1134				// Hard provider pin: reject every other provider identity, even
1135				// when a non-pinned candidate would otherwise score higher.
1136				eligible = false

1153			// unreachable) are dispatchability failures — hard-gate them so the
1154			// router doesn't burn ~30s per cell dialing a host that's already
1155			// known to be off. Route-attempt cooldowns remain demotions (handled
1156			// via inCooldown below) so a single transient failure doesn't break
1157			// sticky-lease continuity; only proactive discovery failure flips
1158			// this hard gate.

1160			// An explicit provider pin bypasses the gate so the operator can
1161			// still reach the provider intentionally.
1162			if eligible && p.Name != "" && in.CooldownDuration > 0 && req.Provider != candidateProviderIdentity(h, p) {

1399	// resolveModel picks the concrete model string for a (harness, provider) pair
1400	// given the request. Returns the model and a non-empty rejection reason if
1401	// resolution fails.
1402	func resolveModel(h HarnessEntry, p ProviderEntry, req Request, in Inputs) (string, string) {
1403		// 1. Exact pin.
1404		if req.Model != "" {
1405			// If the provider has discovery data, try fuzzy matching to map the
1406			// canonical/short ref to the provider-native ID.
1407			if len(p.DiscoveredIDs) > 0 {
1408				if matched := FuzzyMatch(req.Model, p.DiscoveredIDs); matched != "" {
1409					return matched, ""
1410				}
1411				return "", fmt.Sprintf("model %q not on provider %q", req.Model, p.Name)
1412			}
1413			if p.DiscoveryAttempted {
1414				return "", fmt.Sprintf("provider %q has no live discovered models", p.Name)
1415			}
1416			return req.Model, ""
1417		}
1418	
1419		// 2. Provider default → harness default. Empty default is acceptable
1420		// when no request fields constrained model selection — orphan validation
1421		// happens at dispatch time.
1422		if p.DefaultModel != "" {
1423			if len(p.DiscoveredIDs) > 0 {
1424				if matched := FuzzyMatch(p.DefaultModel, p.DiscoveredIDs); matched != "" {
1425					return matched, ""
1426				}
1427				return "", fmt.Sprintf("provider default %q not on provider %q", p.DefaultModel, p.Name)
1428			}
1429			return p.DefaultModel, ""
1430		}
1431		if h.DefaultModel != "" {
1432			return h.DefaultModel, ""
1433		}
1434		return "", ""
1435	}

1437	// EscalatePolicyAware is a helper for policy escalation. Given a request that
1438	// failed at one policy, return the next policy to try, restricted to those
1439	// that have a viable candidate under the current Inputs (i.e., the policy's
1440	// resolved concrete model exists on the request's pinned provider, if any).
1441	//
1442	// Fixes ddx-3c5ba7cc: tier escalation respects --provider affinity.
1443	//
1444	// Returns "" when no further policy is viable.
1445	func EscalatePolicyAware(current string, ladder []string, req Request, in Inputs) string {
1446		startIdx := -1
1447		for i, p := range ladder {
1448			if p == current {
1449				startIdx = i
1450				break
1451			}
1452		}
1453		if startIdx < 0 {
1454			return ""
1455		}
1456		for i := startIdx + 1; i < len(ladder); i++ {
1457			next := ladder[i]
1458			probe := req
1459			probe.Policy = next
1460			if dec, err := Resolve(probe, in); err == nil && dec != nil && dec.Harness != "" {
1461				return next
1462			}
1463		}
1464		return ""
1465	}

1	# Changelog
2	
3	All notable changes to Fizeau are recorded here.
4	Dates use the repo convention (`YYYY-MM-DD`); versions follow semver.
5	
6	## [Unreleased]
7	
8	### Breaking — v0.11 Routing Surface
9	
10	- Routing intent is now policy-based. Public callers use `--policy`,
11	  `--min-power`, and `--max-power`; hard pins remain `--model`, `--provider`,
12	  and `--harness`.
13	- Removed routing flags and service fields for the old route-reference surface:
14	  `--profile`, `--model-ref`, `ModelRef`, `ListProfiles`, `ResolveProfile`,
15	  and `ProfileAliases`.
16	- `fiz policies` and `fiz harnesses` expose the supported v0.11 inspection
17	  surfaces. Route-status JSON uses `policy` and `power_policy`.
18	- The canonical policy names are `cheap`, `default`, `smart`, and
19	  `air-gapped`. The dropped policy/alias names are: `fast`, `code-fast`,
20	  `code-economy`, `code-smart`, `standard`, `local`, `offline`, `code-high`,
21	  and `code-medium`.
22	- Pay-per-token providers are default-deny for automatic routing unless
23	  `include_by_default` opts them in. Explicit pins can still select excluded
24	  providers, but policy requirements such as `air-gapped` / `no_remote` still
25	  win.
26	
27	## [v0.10.9] — 2026-05-06
28	
29	### Changed
30	
31	- Sticky utilization routing now records and replays the evidence used to
32	  choose a destination, including endpoint load and route-health state, so the
33	  public routing projections can explain why a sticky route won.
34	- In-process sticky route leases now back the routing behavior for local model
35	  variants, with provider utilization probes normalized across `llama-server`,
36	  `vllm`, `rapid-mlx`, and `omlx`.
37	- Provider and route-status surfaces now carry the sticky-utilization evidence
38	  needed by the service facade and session replay helpers.
39	
40	### Tests
41	
42	- Coverage was added or refreshed for sticky route selection, route-health
43	  utilization storage, provider utilization probes, and cassette-based
44	  utilization recordings for the local server providers.
45	
46	## [v0.10.8] — 2026-05-05
47	
48	### Changed
49	
50	- Root `package fizeau` is now the public service facade for the cleanup
51	  covered by ADR-008. Concrete execution dispatch, transcript/progress
52	  rendering, route-health feedback, provider quota state, burn-rate tracking,
53	  and routing-quality aggregation now live behind internal package boundaries.
54	- Fizeau owns the public transcript/progress semantics for native and
55	  subprocess harness paths, so downstream consumers can consume service events
56	  and projections without parsing harness-native streams or private session-log
57	  records.
58	- Pure no-op compaction remains silent at the public service boundary; stale
59	  downstream no-op compaction stall policy should migrate away from that signal.
60

1	// Package core contains the internal agent loop and provider-facing DTOs.
2	package core
3	
4	import (
5		"context"
6		"encoding/json"
7		"time"
8	
9		"github.com/easel/fizeau/internal/reasoning"
10		"github.com/easel/fizeau/telemetry"
11	)
12	
13	// Status represents the outcome of a internal agent loop.
14	type Status string
15	
16	const (
17		StatusSuccess        Status = "success"
18		StatusIterationLimit Status = "iteration_limit"
19		StatusCancelled      Status = "cancelled"
20		StatusError          Status = "error"
21		// StatusBudgetHalted indicates the loop halted before issuing the next
22		// llm.request because the configured per-run cost cap (Request.CostCapUSD)
23		// would be exceeded by the projected next turn. The cap is enforced
24		// against the running session total; "projected" uses the most recent
25		// turn's cost as a conservative estimate. Per FEAT-005 §26-29 and
26		// AC-FEAT-005-07, this is a first-class terminal outcome distinct from
27		// completed / cancelled / error.
28		StatusBudgetHalted Status = "budget_halted"
29	)
30	
31	// Role identifies the sender of a message in the internal conversation history.
32	type Role string
33	
34	const (
35		RoleSystem    Role = "system"
36		RoleUser      Role = "user"
37		RoleAssistant Role = "assistant"
38		RoleTool      Role = "tool"
39	)
40	
41	// TokenUsage tracks input and output token counts for the internal loop API.
42	type TokenUsage struct {
43		Input      int `json:"input"`
44		Output     int `json:"output"`
45		CacheRead  int `json:"cache_read,omitempty"`
46		CacheWrite int `json:"cache_write,omitempty"`
47		Total      int `json:"total"`
48		// Reasoning is the count of thinking-mode tokens included in Output.
49		// Servers that expose `usage.completion_tokens_details.reasoning_tokens`
50		// (OpenAI o1/o3/gpt-5, DeepSeek R1, Qwen3, ds4) populate this from the
51		// upstream wire field. Output already includes reasoning tokens — this
52		// is a sub-count for analyses that need to separate "model thought" from
53		// "model wrote the answer". Zero when the provider doesn't expose it.
54		Reasoning int `json:"reasoning,omitempty"`
55		// ReasoningTokensApprox is true when Reasoning was estimated from
56		// message.reasoning_content char-count÷4 because
57		// usage.completion_tokens_details.reasoning_tokens was absent. The
58		// char-count/4 estimator is a v1 placeholder; accuracy degrades for
59		// non-ASCII-heavy content. When false (or absent), Reasoning is
60		// provider-reported and authoritative.
61		ReasoningTokensApprox bool `json:"reasoning_tokens_approx,omitempty"`
62	}
63	
64	// Add accumulates token counts from another TokenUsage.
65	func (u *TokenUsage) Add(other TokenUsage) {
66		u.Input += other.Input
67		u.Output += other.Output
68		u.CacheRead += other.CacheRead
69		u.CacheWrite += other.CacheWrite
70		u.Total += other.Total
71		u.Reasoning += other.Reasoning
72		u.ReasoningTokensApprox = u.ReasoningTokensApprox || other.ReasoningTokensApprox
73	}
74	
75	// ToolCall represents a tool invocation requested by the model in the internal
76	// loop API.
77	type ToolCall struct {
78		ID        string          `json:"id"`
79		Name      string          `json:"name"`
80		Arguments json.RawMessage `json:"arguments"`
81	}
82	
83	// Message is a single message in the internal conversation history.
84	type Message struct {
85		Role       Role       `json:"role"`
86		Content    string     `json:"content,omitempty"`
87		ToolCalls  []ToolCall `json:"tool_calls,omitempty"`
88		ToolCallID string     `json:"tool_call_id,omitempty"`
89	}
90	
91	// ToolDef describes a tool for the internal LLM provider interface.
92	type ToolDef struct {
93		Name        string          `json:"name"`
94		Description string          `json:"description"`
95		Parameters  json.RawMessage `json:"parameters"` // JSON Schema
96	}
97	
98	type Reasoning = reasoning.Reasoning
99	
100	const (
101		ReasoningAuto    = reasoning.ReasoningAuto
102		ReasoningOff     = reasoning.ReasoningOff
103		ReasoningLow     = reasoning.ReasoningLow
104		ReasoningMedium  = reasoning.ReasoningMedium
105		ReasoningHigh    = reasoning.ReasoningHigh
106		ReasoningMinimal = reasoning.ReasoningMinimal
107		ReasoningXHigh   = reasoning.ReasoningXHigh
108		ReasoningMax     = reasoning.ReasoningMax
109	)
110	
111	func ReasoningTokens(n int) Reasoning {
112		return reasoning.ReasoningTokens(n)
113	}
114	
115	// Options configures a single internal provider Chat call.
116	//
117	// SeamOptions is embedded to carry test injection seams when the testseam
118	// build tag is set; in production builds it is an empty struct with no fields.
119	type Options struct {
120		SeamOptions
121	
122		Model       string   `json:"model,omitempty"`
123		Temperature *float64 `json:"temperature,omitempty"`
124		TopP        *float64 `json:"top_p,omitempty"`
125		TopK        *int     `json:"top_k,omitempty"`
126		MinP        *float64 `json:"min_p,omitempty"`
127		// RepetitionPenalty is the OpenAI-compat field name; openai-compat
128		// servers (omlx, lmstudio, vLLM, OpenRouter) accept it as a top-level
129		// extra. >1.0 discourages exact repeats; 1.05–1.1 is typical for Qwen.
130		RepetitionPenalty *float64 `json:"repetition_penalty,omitempty"`
131		Seed              int64    `json:"seed,omitempty"`
132		MaxTokens         int      `json:"max_tokens,omitempty"`
133		Stop              []string `json:"stop,omitempty"`
134		// SamplingSource is a comma-separated record of which resolution layers
135		// supplied non-nil sampler fields, e.g. "catalog" or
136		// "catalog,provider_config". Used only for the llm.request telemetry
137		// event; never sent on the provider wire. See ADR-007 §5.
138		SamplingSource string `json:"sampling_source,omitempty"`
139		// Reasoning controls model-side reasoning with one scalar value. Empty means
140		// unset; use ReasoningOff or ReasoningTokens(0) for explicit off.
141		Reasoning Reasoning `json:"reasoning,omitempty"`
142	
143		// CachePolicy is the public prompt-caching opt-out plumbed from
144		// ServiceExecuteRequest. Valid values: "" / "default" / "off". Providers
145		// that implement caching (currently Anthropic, via cache_control writes
146		// in a follow-up bead) consult this field; providers without caching
147		// ignore it.
148		CachePolicy string `json:"cache_policy,omitempty"`
149	}

151	// Response is the result of a single internal provider Chat call.
152	type Response struct {
153		Content      string           `json:"content"`
154		ToolCalls    []ToolCall       `json:"tool_calls,omitempty"`
155		Usage        TokenUsage       `json:"usage"`
156		Model        string           `json:"model"`
157		FinishReason string           `json:"finish_reason"`
158		Attempt      *AttemptMetadata `json:"attempt,omitempty"`
159	}
160	
161	// Provider is the internal interface that LLM backends implement.
162	type Provider interface {
163		Chat(ctx context.Context, messages []Message, tools []ToolDef, opts Options) (Response, error)
164	}
165	
166	// Tool is the internal interface that agent tools implement.
167	type Tool interface {
168		// Name returns the tool's identifier.
169		Name() string
170		// Description returns a human-readable description for the LLM.
171		Description() string
172		// Schema returns the JSON Schema for the tool's parameters.
173		Schema() json.RawMessage
174		// Execute runs the tool with the given parameters and returns the result.
175		Execute(ctx context.Context, params json.RawMessage) (string, error)
176		// Parallel reports whether this tool is safe to execute concurrently with
177		// other parallel-flagged tools. Read-only tools return true; tools with
178		// side effects (writes, shell commands, sub-agents) return false.
179		Parallel() bool
180	}
181	
182	// ToolCallLog records one tool execution during a internal agent loop.
183	type ToolCallLog struct {
184		Tool     string          `json:"tool"`
185		Input    json.RawMessage `json:"input"`
186		Output   string          `json:"output"`
187		Duration time.Duration   `json:"duration_ms"`
188		Error    string          `json:"error,omitempty"`
189	}
190	
191	// EventType identifies the kind of event emitted during a internal agent loop.
192	//
193	// uses ServiceEvent.
194	type EventType string
195	
196	const (
197		EventSessionStart    EventType = "session.start"
198		EventLLMRequest      EventType = "llm.request"
199		EventLLMResponse     EventType = "llm.response"
200		EventToolCall        EventType = "tool.call"
201		EventSessionEnd      EventType = "session.end"
202		EventLLMDelta        EventType = "llm.delta"
203		EventCompactionStart EventType = "compaction.start"
204		EventCompactionEnd   EventType = "compaction.end"
205		// EventOverride and EventRejectedOverride mirror the service-stream

208		// metrics across restarts and beyond the in-memory ring's retention.
209		EventOverride         EventType = "override"
210		EventRejectedOverride EventType = "rejected_override"
211		// EventReasoningStall fires immediately before consumeStream returns
212		// ErrReasoningStall. Its data payload carries model, timeout_ms,
213		// reasoning_tail, and prompt_id so harnesses and dashboards can count
214		// stall rate and debug what the model was reasoning about at the time.
215		EventReasoningStall EventType = "reasoning.stall"
216		// EventPlanningTurn fires after the planning LLM call completes when

219		EventPlanningTurn EventType = "planning.turn"

input.new_string:
## [Unreleased]

### Added

- `RouteRequest.ExcludedRoutes` (`[]ExcludedRoute`): callers can now supply a
  list of `(Provider, Model, Endpoint)` combinations to exclude from routing.
  The router skips any candidate matching an entry and records it with the new
  `FilterReasonCallerExcluded` (`"caller_excluded"`) filter reason, so
  routing-quality observability can distinguish caller-driven re-routes from
  internal health signals. `Model` and `Endpoint` are optional — an empty value
  matches any. Propagated from the public `ResolveRoute` surface through to the
  internal routing engine.

### Fixed

- **Connect-time fast-fail**: dial-level TCP failures (ECONNREFUSED, ETIMEDOUT,
  EHOSTUNREACH — detected as `*net.OpError{Op: "dial"}`) no longer run through
  the 4-attempt exponential retry curve. A dead local endpoint now fails in
  under 5 s instead of burning ~2 m 45 s of wall-clock per call. In-flight
  5xx / rate-limit errors retain the existing retry behavior.

### Breaking — v0.11 Routing Surface

**AC-1 & AC-2 — `RouteRequest.ExcludedRoutes`:**
- Added `ExcludedRoute` struct (`Provider`, `Model`, `Endpoint`) to `service.go`
